title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Contract Lens: Host Port Allowlist
summary: An origin trusts arbitrary Host and X-Forwarded values, builds reset links from the request, rate-limits the proxy address, and logs raw URLs containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts host port allowlist increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
